title: Rabbit Hole
description: Exploring personal growth and existential exploration.
lyrics: The rabbit hole is a funny experience, right? The rabbit hole is something of a tumultuous step that you gotta take, right? The idea that you could hide in there and it could be a sanctuary, or you could hop in there and you could get eaten by the monster that's taking over your home. It's a dark, dark, dark place that you either find sanctuary in or you find death in, right? And then, I don't know, it's almost like when you come out of the vault and fall out, you know? Light's pretty bright, the world looks different, and maybe you understand things or view it in a different perspective. I'm always, in my whole entire life up to this moment, I'm always looking for rabbit holes. Those situations, those moments that challenge me, that make me view something in a completely different way than I viewed it before. And I hope I survive all of those moments. Metaphorically, you can put that anywhere in your life, but I think that's what Lewis Carroll was talking about, when Alice basically goes down the rabbit hole and meets a rabbit. What kind of trip is that? Who are you, Alice in Wonderland? Or are you just Alice in your own land and now nobody even understands that you've been through Wonderland, you know? And maybe you go back to those experiences. Maybe you go back to being like, I hope I'm in Wonderland again, you know? Because it taught me so much about who I am that I'm going to go back into that rabbit hole. I'm going to look for another one because I don't think I'm fully formed. When are you fully formed? When is anybody? I think the closest you can get to being fully formed, in my opinion, is the moment before you're going to die. Or the moment before you're born. Okay, yeah, but we don't remember that one, so I have no recollection. Exactly, that's how close we are to that. No doubt. I mean, our time in life is short, right? But you don't remember it, but you remember the rabbit hole. So what I'm saying is, I think a point where you get that you're not going to search for another rabbit hole and you indifference is the moment that you die. Maybe it's not physically, right? Maybe it's not like I'm dying physically. That's another moment. If you search for rabbit holes all the time, then you're going to... But you can definitely die. And still be alive. Mentally and emotionally. When you stop searching for the things that are going to make you reevaluate your existence and be... Well, then there's also the difference of going down the rabbit hole and learning a whole bunch for your individual self and then jumping in the rabbit hole again with, say, I don't know, a partner? Okay, but that... That's a whole different rabbit hole. Well, no, I mean, it's not even a rabbit hole because it's still a rabbit hole. Because a rabbit hole only fits one rabbit. That's the point of it. It's a hole that's built by one rabbit. Oh my gosh! That goes into the hole and it finds it and it comes out and it's a different person and it can now relate to other rabbits that have been down holes like that. Either that or you go down the hole and you get eaten by a snake. Because if a snake is taking itself up in your hole, then you're going to go find it to reevaluate yourself. I mean, the dying part would be totally... You don't know if that's going to happen to any rabbit hole. Even if you take your car up the street. So taking your car for a drive is a rabbit hole?
